The paper explores two embedding techniques: word embeddings and second–order co–occurrence vectors. Word embeddings are a type of distributional technique that uses a vector representation of a word based on its co–occurrence with other words. Second–order co–occurrence vectors are a type of distributional technique that uses a vector representation of a word based on its co–occurrence with other words.
